segments.text: Then Jonah prayed to the Lord his God from the belly of the fish.
In my agony I cried out to the Lord and he answered me; from the depths of Sheol I pleaded for help and you answered me.
segments.speaker: Jonah

segments.text: You threw me into the deep, all the way down to the bottom of the sea.  Water flooded all around me; your crashing waves rolled over me.
segments.speaker: Jonah

segments.text: I said to myself, ‘The Lord has banished me from his sight. Will I ever see your holy Temple again?’
segments.speaker: Jonah

segments.text: The water swirled over me so I couldn't breathe; the deep sucked me down; seaweed wrapped itself around my head.
segments.speaker: Jonah

segments.text: I sank down to the base of the mountains; the earth barred me in forever. But you, my Lord, my God, brought me back up from the abyss.
segments.speaker: Jonah

segments.text: As my life ebbed away, I remembered the Lord; my prayer came to you in your holy Temple.
segments.speaker: Jonah

segments.text: Those who worship worthless idols give up their trust in God's goodness.
segments.speaker: Jonah

segments.text: But I will offer you sacrifices, shouting out my thanks. I will keep my promises to you, for salvation comes from the Lord.
segments.speaker: Jonah

segments.text: Then the Lord told the fish to spit out Jonah onto the shore.
